title: Middle East conflict strands travellers and drives up fuel prices | 7NEWS
description: Middle East conflict disrupts travel and fuel supplies for Australians. Thousands of travellers are stranded at airports with flights cancelled through Dubai, Abu Dhabi and Doha, affecting approximately 3,500 flights and one million passengers globally. Fuel prices are expected to rise by 33 cents per litre by week's end due to supply concerns through the Strait of Hormuz, where 20% of the world's oil travels. title: South Australia promises to scrap public school fees | 7NEWS
description: South Australian Labor leader Peter Malinauskas has pledged to eliminate public school fees if re-elected, a policy that would provide cost of living relief to 90,000 families across the state. The measure would abolish fees for materials and services in public primary and high schools, which currently cost up to $700 annually, at an estimated cost of $300 million over four years. This would be the first policy of its kind implemented in Australia, though it would not cover additional expenses such as excursions, uniforms and sporting equipment. title: Inside Operation Epic Fury | United States and Israel attack Iran | Special 7NEWS coverage
description: A special bulletin covering the latest updates on the unfolding conflict in Iran and the Middle East after the United States and Israel attacked on Saturday under Operation Epic Fury, targeting the nation’s top leaders and calling for the overthrow of its government.   The attacks have sparked retaliatory strikes on the Israeli city of Tel Aviv, where 7NEWS Chief Reporter Chris Reason has been reporting live as the situation continues to shift.   Reason and his team captured the moment an Iranian missile breached Israel’s air-defence system, the Iron Dome, before crashing into a building and lighting up the city with a massive explosion.   He was joined by 7NEWS US correspondents Rob Scott in Washington DC and Mylee Hogan in the US Bureau, alongside 7NEWS Foreign Editor Hugh Whitfeld, providing insight and analysis on the fast‑developing international crisis.   7NEWS Aviation Editor Blake Johnson covered grounded flights and travel chaos, with Dubai airport struck and planes carrying Australians to Europe turned around, while Europe Correspondent Jacquelin Robson provided live updates on how European leaders are responding to the global conflict, and Political Editor Mark Riley in Canberra with the latest on Australia’s position. title: PM says Aussie troops won't be sent to Iran
description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has stressed that Australian troops won't be deployed into Iran following extensive Israel-US strikes across the country. #troops #iran #middleeast #deployment #defence

title: Petrol prices set to soar following Iran strikes
description: Australians are bracing for a potential petrol price surge of up to 40 per cent in coming weeks as the escalating Middle East conflict threatens global oil supplies.  The crisis has already begun applying upward pressure on fuel prices, with all eyes now on the Strait of Hormuz, the world’s most critical chokepoint for oil and gas exports. #petrol #iran #oil #fuel #middleeast

title: Family of teen who died on K'gari call for inquest
description: The family of 19-year-old Canadian backpacker Piper James, who was found dead on K'gari in January, have called for an inquest into her death. #kgari #piperjames #dingo #inquest #qld

title: Three US service members killed
description: The United States Central Command has confirmed three fatalities during an ongoing major conflict with Iran.  Taking to social media on Sunday, local time, the command said a further “five are seriously wounded as part of operation Epic Fury”. #iran #middleeast #conflict #usa #military

title: Triple-negative breast cancer warning for young women | 7NEWS
description: Triple-negative breast cancer is a fast-growing form of the disease that is frequently missed by routine screening, particularly in women under 40 who are not included in the BreastScreen Australia program. Health advocates are urging women to trust their instincts and seek immediate medical follow-up for any new or persistent breast changes. The awareness push coincides with Breast Cancer Awareness Day. title: Super gap, school fees and pet etiquette debated | 7NEWS

A debate has emerged over pet owner etiquette, with one author criticising dog owners who carry waste bags into cafes and public spaces. title: What happens next in the Middle East as conflict intensifies
description: The conflict in the Middle East is escalating in the wake of joint attacks by Israel and the US on Iran. Donald Trump is now warning of more American casualties as the Iranian President brags of "crushing enemy bases." Middle East analyst with the Lowy Institute, Dr Rodger Shanahan, explains the situation in the region and how Iran will choose a new supreme leader after the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. #iran #israel #mideast #middleeast #worldnews
